task_completion.description: Open the multispecies FASTA file and select the human hemoglobin beta sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing 'hemoglobin_beta_multispecies.fasta' loaded, with the sequence for 'HUMAN' or 'P68871' selected or highlighted in the Sequence View.

task_completion.description: Perform an in silico trypsin digest on the human sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE 'In silico digestion' dialog or results panel visible with 'Trypsin' selected as the enzyme, or cleavage site annotations appearing on the sequence.

task_completion.description: Export the annotated human sequence in GenBank format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE 'Export sequence' dialog showing GenBank format selected and the output path set to '~/UGENE_Data/proteomics_results/hbb_trypsin_annotated.gb', or terminal/file manager confirming the file creation.

task_completion.description: Identify the resulting peptide fragments from the digest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing the list of generated fragments, their lengths, and sequences in the annotations table or digest results panel.

task_completion.description: Write a summary report containing the three longest fragments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation of '~/UGENE_Data/proteomics_results/trypsin_fragments_report.txt' containing the sequences FFESFGDLSTPDAVMGNPK, VLGAFSDGLAHLDNLK, and LLGNVLVCVLAHHFGK.

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE GUI to perform the digest.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the UGENE application open and being interacted with for the digestion step, rather than the agent using a command-line tool or writing a Python script to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the fragment sequences from the UGENE interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent viewing the fragment list in UGENE before or while writing the report, proving they didn't just hallucinate or copy-paste the answer from external knowledge.

integrity.description: The agent saved the files to the exact requested directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: File save dialogs, text editor save prompts, or terminal commands showing the exact paths '/home/ga/UGENE_Data/proteomics_results/hbb_trypsin_annotated.gb' and '/home/ga/UGENE_Data/proteomics_results/trypsin_fragments_report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output paths are /home/ga/UGENE_Data/proteomics_results/hbb_trypsin_annotated.gb and /home/ga/UGENE_Data/proteomics_results/trypsin_fragments_report.txt. The three longest trypsin fragments for human HBB (P68871) are FFESFGDLSTPDAVMGNPK (19-mer), VLGAFSDGLAHLDNLK (16-mer), and LLGNVLVCVLAHHFGK (16-mer). The VLM should verify these exact sequences are present in the final report.
